rag_rules: For implementation contracts, use assign only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable.
AGREE contracts must be placed in the correct annex scope: component type annexes for assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for bindings.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the input AADL model.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all identifiers are visible in the current scope and match the AADL model structure.
Avoid referencing implementation-only subcomponents in component type annexes; use only visible ports and features.
If a guarantee references an unresolved identifier or invalid helper, remove the clause or fix the root reference.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Guarantee expressions must reference valid, typed identifiers visible in the current scope.
Use only visible AADL features or declared equation helpers in the same annex.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign for implementation-level bindings.
Do not reference data types or identifiers not declared in the AADL model or its configured dependencies.
